---
subtitle: "Tools 1"
title: "Day 1 - Block 4"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D1 B4"
    bibliography: ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Tools make agents useful {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Good tools, good checks, good outcomes.
:::

## Objective

- Understand MCP and skills
- Compare RAG, skill, MCP
- Run one verified tool task

## Inputs

- Coding agent with tools
- Sample data or codebase
- One submission form link

## Deliverable

- One tool-run trace
- One verified output artifact
- One uncertainty note

## Timebox

- 20 min lecture input
- 30 min hands-on build
- 10 min debrief share-outs

## Submission link

- Exercise ID: E3
- URL: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E3`
- One spokesperson per group

## Evaluation rubric

- Correctness of output
- Verification evidence quality
- Constraint compliance discipline

## Extension task

- Add second tool safely
- Add retry and fallback
- Report residual risk clearly

## What is a skill?

- Reusable workflow instruction pack
- Loaded by explicit command
- Great for repeated tasks

## What is MCP?

- Standardized tool integration layer
- Agent calls external systems
- Permissions define safety boundary

## RAG vs Skill vs MCP

| Mechanism | Best use | Common failure |
|---|---|---|
| RAG | missing knowledge context | stale retrieval |
| Skill | repeatable workflow steps | blind copy use |
| MCP | external action execution | permission mismatch |

## Tool loop that works

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Task] --> B[Pick mechanism]
  B --> C[Plan]
  C --> D[Execute tool]
  D --> E[Verify result]
  E --> F[Record trace]
  E -->|fails| C
```

## Hands-on menu

- Install one useful skill
- Run one arXiv paper search
- Edit one spreadsheet via Python

## Debrief

- What failed first today?
- What evidence raised confidence?
- What stayed human-owned?

## Resource anchors {.smaller}

- R004 agentic engineering patterns
- R003 RAG skill MCP map
- R012 coding assistance evidence
